I have been using TravelPro luggage almost exclusively for over 10 years now. However, the largest size I have has been well used and so I was hoping to find a replacement for it. When I saw this Fanskey Luggage, I had never heard of the brand, but I figured I would try it. This is a 3 piece set with spinner wheels. The quality is as good as TravelPro and I actually might say I think it is better. The cases maneuver well and are very lightweight. I love the rich dark red color as well (I don't see much luggage this color so it makes it easier when I was at the airport over the holidays to spot). The three sizes fit within each other for easy storage. Highly recommend!

Sadly, this jacket was ruined by the terribly sewn inner lining, especially around the tag.  It's so awful It would be hard to sell at an super cut outlet. Further the seller appears to have known this when they sent it out stating that it didn't seem to effect the wear. Seriously? Not for this price.  I have included pics for you to see. I did return the jacket immediately.  I do feel compelled however to share my thoughts about the jacket 1.  The fur appears to be mink.  It is not very soft.. but that is because the mink has not been sheared, so the stiffer, longer hairs are still present. I own several very high quality minks and I usually choose the sheared pelts as they are so much softer. The color is good.  Fairly deep and rich looking.  The pic I included was with full sun on it to show the pelts and the knit better. 2.  This jacket is "knitted" using remnants of mink pelts. The "knitting" means there is a slight "give" or stretch to the jacket. The sizing to me appears to be spot on.  I did go one size larger in order to accommodate sweaters underneath.  I had considered traveling with this jacket. 3.  The inner lining is a very, very cheap crepe. After consulting with my furrier*, the quality of the crepe was so inferior in order to "repair" the lining sewing it would necessary to completely remove the lining and start over.  Far more cost than the value of the jacket.  He also advised me that the low quality of the lining material is such that it will tear and not bear up to regular wear.  *Furrier is my storage, care and alteration specialist - NOT a fur salesmen. 4. The sleeves are extraordinarily long.  I actually liked this, however, you should know you cannot fold the sleeve over as to the lining and sewing construction of the jacket. So the sleeves fell almost down to my finger tips. You can kind of see this on the model wearing the jacket. 5.  The "crush" collar does in fact create a floppy hood of sorts, but is sticks out in odd angles because of the "shoulder" humps that keep the collar down around your shoulders when it is not a hood. 6.  The jacket has fur hooks for closures down the front and at the neck if you decide to pull the hood up and use as a hood. 7.  It claims to be, and SORT of is, a semi swing coat... meaning wider at the bottom than at the chest... sort of A shaped... HOWEVER.. the hem is so horribly sewn that it is terribly uneven all around. In fact, the hem looks as though it is longer at both hips and then shorter in the front and back.  One side being longer than other.  Further, it was quite long.  Falling well below my knees and I'm 5'7". Over all, it was just horrible construction. Dealing with this seller has been challenging as well. I will not be a return customer.  Amazon had to step in to help facilitate the return.  Shouldn't have been necessary as it was a straight up, immediate return. Because I personally use verified purchase reviews when I purchase items, I do my very best to provide an honest, verified purchase review and include pictures when I can. I respond to all questions/comments.  I never hesitate to update a review if new info should seem useful.  As I returned the item, there will likely not be any additional information.

First things first.... I am about 5'8".. usually wear a 14/16, 38DDD and my body shape is a curvy hour glass.  This robe is a beautiful, rich, deep navy and very, very thin. The tie is sewn on.  There are pockets on both sides.  There is an inside tie as well to help keep the robe closed.  However, I will say that it is very, very low cut even when tied, in case that matters to you.  The material is very soft... however, SUPER thin... glad I didn't go with one of the lighter colors. You kind of want a robe to be a bit more coverage - or at least I do. I bought it to take when I travel because it's lightweight (and it is) and easy to wash, which it appears to be.  I gentle washed it and very low temp dried it.  I would say that it is a bit smaller than I would like particularly on the top, but that is usually a problem I have and don't necessarily hold that against the robe.  Wish there was a bit more material on top and that it stayed closed a bit better.  Probably would not buy again.

It lists in the description "chiffon" - this blouse is NOT chiffon in any way shape or form.  It is a poly/stretch material. But I already knew that just by the photos of the blouse... I am 5'7" ..  14/16 or 0x/1x and 38 E/F.  Because I do not like a super snug fit over my bust I sized up to the 2x.  After trying it on, I would say I would need a 3x.  The arms and the body fit fine in the 2x - body loose, arms snug but not overly.  However, the pocket "flaps" stick straight out because the blouse is too snug over the bust!  Yuck.  The zipper front is interesting but the zipper is a really rough, cheap looking and feeling closure.  Eh.  I could tack the pocket flaps down (there are NO pockets - just flaps) - but that would look really cheap to me.  On the up side, the material was ok.  The print was Fall like to me in color.  The sleeves were a perfect 3/4 on my body.  It would be a great blouse to travel with because it would not wrinkle. I did not wash it as I left tags on.  It is a totally machine wash/tumble dry material though.  I would worry a bit about the quality of the zipper though.  I wish it were a better quality.  Overall, I cannot ignore the flaps that stick straight out that look like landing notices or something.  Not a really classy look.  Over all I would say size up at least 2 sizes if you have a fuller bust as I do.  I'm going to offer it to a friend who is less endowed and see if she likes it at all. Having said all of that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

First, this is a TWO piece set, NOT a 3 piece robe, chemise and shorts.  It's ONLY the robe and chemise.  I am 5'7", usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x, I am a 38E/F.  With those numbers and reading reviews, I chose the xxlarge. It was exactly right.  The chemise has adjustable straps with a lot of adjustability - numbers of inches.  It's a bit shorter than I would like, above my knee with straps adjusted about 3".  The robe is roomy, with an inner strap to tie and then the side straps that are sewn on each side - which I LOVE.  I HATE a loose tie.  The bummer is that there are NO pockets!  That is really ... Meh.  But it's not a deal breaker.  I purchased to take on a cruise and I was looking for something that didn't wrinkle.  This is a PERFECT liquid nylon knit that is very easy to wash, dry and just throw into a suitcase.  I love the black/flower print.  I preferred one of the other black flower prints, but the smaller flower print was available in the xxlarge and the sizing is way more important to me. It is a slinky, comfortable and attractive on in my opinion.  I think it will be a terrific travel piece, however, with no pockets - I hesitate to purchase any others.  Also, even tho the listing notes it is a 3 pc set, it is NOT.  It is 2 pc, robe and chemise.  Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. DISLIKES - When I walk with them I feel like I have to exert a lot of foot muscle strength just to keep them on my feet and my feet get tired.  - The arch support is good but not great.  - Because they feel airy, it feels like these will wear out rapidly.  - They make my feet sweat like clogs.  - The red color I ordered comes off on my feet and/or socks  In summary, I would not purchase them again had I known what it would feel like to wear them for a few blocks. UPDATE BELOW.....Wow.  These suitcases are AWESOME for the price!  I was expecting a lot less.  I purchased 3.  I chose the bright, safety orange.  Easy to see in the sea of other colors for sure. The outer shell is hard - sturdy feeling.  The inside is roomy.  They are around 5-6" deep in each side (I've included some pics - sorry about holding the ruler upside down - but you can see it's about 5 to 6")  One side is zipper closed, the other side has elastic straps to contain contents.  The outer zippers are sturdy feeling and run smoothly.  There is also an 'extender' zipper giving you an extra 2" or so in depth.  Honestly, these suitcases 'feel' as nice as some others I have purchased for literally 4x the price!  All the nice care points too with the inner zipper, and additional pockets.  It looks and feels like a suitcase way beyond the price point.  Now, in all fairness, they are new to me and I will be using them on a trip in about a week.  So, I will update my review should new info seem useful.  The ad says they weigh just under 10lbs.. I used my handheld suitcase scale and it registered 9.1 lbs empty.  One of the pics I included shows me holding a 12" ruler so you can get an idea of the width of the inside of the cases.  The pull out handle is not as 'sturdy' feeling as I would like... but it works- It locks down and lifts easily.  It appears or feels like the pullout handle is plastic at the handle part.. the bar appears to be lightweight aluminum - so it might be bent with lots of use.. we'll see.  There are two grab handles one on the top and one the side of case.  The outer wheels are multi-directional... and stick out quite a bit and I'm not sure they are as 'sturdy' as the rest of the case.  If there is an 'Achilles heel' on these bags... the wheels may be it or possibly the pull handle.. but we as I said, time will tell and I never hesitate to update.  I've included some pics and hope my review was helpful to you. UPDATE:  12/4/2019.... Have taken these suitcases through the airline system two times so far... NO problems to report.  I am happier and happier with this purchase... but time will tell and I update if new info seem useful.

UPDATE: 6/20/2016....  Sadly, a stone popped out.  Had hoped this would last at least 6 mos.  :(  It was very comfortable to wear... BUT it did snag things on the settings... and of course, with the stone gone, it's a cheap looking piece of junk jewelry - no longer "believable" in way shape or form.  Eh.  I HATE to update reviews when it's no good.  I do NOT recommend this piece any longer.  Sorry.... On first blush, this ring seems very worth the less than 10 bucks (ring + shipping - actually more than the ring cost).  It is comfortable to wear so far. I was nervous that the double band effect would be uncomfortable between fingers, however, I quickly got used to the ring and don't notice it at all now. The back of the ring narrows just the right amount to make bending and clutching your hand easy, comfortable. Also, the bands themselves seems strong... able to take a bit of pressure without bending - although I don't recommend a stress test to try the theory.  Just pinching them between my fingers with some pretty good strength didn't do anything to the bands. The stones are surprisingly sparkly considering how tiny they are.  I have to say, however, it is a bit rough over the stone area due to the stone setting, so I would use caution wearing it with fine silks, sweaters and other snaggy material.  The plating seems bright and I think it looks like a lot more than the price paid in my opinion. Definitely "believable" as white gold and real diamonds - however, it is plated silver and CZ's just to be clear. I will be updating should there be any wear or other issues to report.  It arrived quickly and in good condition.

I was really excited when I saw this sweatshirt… I’ve been searching for the marled look and these pictures were just that! However, upon receiving.. I was met with a sheer pink poly fabric that gave off a smooth, sheen look and was the exact opposite of what I wanted. This fabric is nothing as expected nor, I would want in look or, feel. I included photos for those who may be interested so, you’ll have an accurate photo of what this is… The peachy pink are photos taken from the actual top that arrived- the marled pink is the posted photographs. Hopefully this helps eliminate any unwanted surprises.  Material is thin. Although, described as a sweatshirt-  I Personally think the word sweatshirt gets used loosely in descriptions.. this does have a banded collar and waistband- along with cuffed sleeves. But, a sweatshirt in my opinion has a certain amount of weight and substance to it.. maybe even a little bit of bulk- this does not! This falls more in line of a long sleeve shirt that has a cuffed waistband.  As for the fit, this does offer some stretch. I find it runs true to U.S. sizes. It seems a lot of designs are using synthetic poly- rayon-spandex mix. IMHO, It’s not a very appealing fabric, it tends to feel and look cheap. But, I do realize this is truly a personal choice! For reference: I’m 5’2”, 154lb, 38C, 40+ yr. old with my trouble spot thru the middle (thx to x4 kids and gravity.. ugh!!) I hope this info provides ability to compare sizing- which, I ordered XL. The largest part of this fit was in the length! **Also.. If not wanting shrinkage- I’d suggest line drying as, the material will shrink!  Overall, being lighter weight and thinner synthetics- I would suggest this is best for spring/fall as, its not going to give a lot of substantial warmth unless, it is layered up! At a price point approx $25 you get a thinner fabric that is closely made to resemble a sweatshirt. Unfortunately, I was hard-pressed to find a lot of things right that I liked about it- it just wasn’t for me. I hope this info can help your decision… Feel free to let me know!

First off, I'm a 5'7", 175lb, 36DD, athletically built woman (aka: I've got meat on my bones)  This swimsuit is utterly laughable. I am giving it 2 stars for being, for the most part, well made (i.e., no loose threads, no bunches of threads, good hems, decent stitching, etc.) and for being very true to the color chosen (I chose the green and it looks *identical* to the green pictured). That's about it for positives.  I followed the provided size chart and man, was I wrong by doing that! I went with a Large when I should've gone with a small. This suit is so loose, I cannot even comment on the supposed tummy control aspect.  And it claims to have a "full coverage back".. yeah, right! This suit is the closest I have ever come in my life to wearing a thong.. once I had it on and went to adjust the straps.. as soon as I hiked the straps tighter, that action sent the "full coverage back" portion right between my cheeks! Yowzer! That part alone would keep me from trying a different size!  (Full coverage) Bottom line... if you want to try this suit, know that the stitching is well done and the color (at least the green) is as pictured but I strongly urge you to size down! But I do warn you (again!) that this is NOT a suit that will provide you with full coverage! If you have *any* amount of junk in your trunk, this suit will be more like a high-cut hip-hugger or, worse yet, an all out thong!

I’ll start by saying I love this robe!  I truly do. Luckily I’d read other reviews and although normally I would immediately wash every item of clothing I purchase I decided to check the sewing and seams first. That said the quality assurance person should be fired. As you can see in the photos 2 seams are already coming undone as they were not seen properly.  There is also what I call “bobbin vomit” in several places where gobs of thread are in and around the seams and in other places there are just loose threads.  Some of the seams do not match up either. If it wasn’t for the seams that are already coming undone it is something I would normally overlook as it was close enough.  The other issue is the size. I’m 5’ 10” tall and I ordered the large/extra large as I’d read some complaints about size and especially short sleeve length. Let me tell you these sleeves will hold monkey arms!  I rolled them up twice!  They never happens to me with a large or extra large robe which I always buy big just to get the longer sleeve length. The length of time is excellent too as it is down between my calves and my ankles. I love the color. I love the robe, but due to the sewing errors as is bound to happen in mass manufacturing I am sending it back. I wish they had the sizes separated by small, medium, large, and extra large not lumping them s/m and l/xl.  I haven’t decided if I’m going to try the robe in a size smaller or just try a different robe.  If this robe was free of sewing mistakes I would have just kept it for a taller friend of mine.  It is the perfect weight. It’s not too heavy and it’s not too light. It is soft. It is fluffy. The pockets are nice and the sayin trummis a nice touch I love the hood. It was the deciding factor in my choosing this robe in the first place.  I’ll be sad to see it go as it really is an excellent robe if it didn’t have the bad seams in 2 places and the Bobbin vomit thread issues.  It’s the kind of robe you will want to snuggle and cuddle into and with someone. Two people will easily fit in this one!  I’m just afraid that if I buy the s/m that the arms will not be long enough and the robe length will be shorter too. I also hate that most s/m robe belts hit me under my breasts instead of at my natural waist and the pockets are located under each breast instead of along my hips. This robe belt fits perfectly at my waist and the pockets sit perfectly at my hips with no awkward belting or pocket maneuvering involved.  I’m really going to miss this robe!  I suspect I will end up with another one shortly, but hopefully one without flaws.

UPDATE BELOW 6/26/2018.... If you HAVE been using the Vanity Fair front closing - 75339, BUT you have been WISHING for more coverage - THIS version is slightly better. I've been wearing the other version for many, many years.  I keep asking for and WISHING for a 38DDD - but to no avail so I buy the 38DD.  Why?  Because I find it comfortable and NOT over padded.  I like a thin lining for comfort, but don't desire additional "size" with the filling.  This version gives you ALMOST 1 more inch in the cup at the small size... at least on the 38DD.  If you wore the 38DD in the "lighter" version, I would recommend buying the same size of this "fuller" version.  I still really need a 38DDD, but I can definitely make this work.  This version IS convertible to a racer back, just like the older version - although I rarely use that option, it's nice to have.  Other improvements include - slightly wider - different material straps.  FAR less "cutting" into the top of your shoulder - further - the back strap is slightly wider.. The front closure is still plastic - but I have yet to have one "fail."  The down side of front closure bra is that as you wear it, as with ALL bras, the elastic "gives" and with the front closure, there are no adjustments.  Still I get a fair amount of wear out each one - more than a year.  For the price, I think this good.  Camel Camel these so that you can grab them when they are an even better price - which has always beat what they are offered to me locally.  There is a thin fiber fill - but it doesn't add bulk - just modesty.  And the cup is smooth - there is some strategically placed lace that doesn't create lumps and bumps on the bra - a pretty touch.  The other think I notice is that this newer version "feels" more supportive.  I think there is a visible lift - meaning more support.  The straps are adjustable, and as I said above, much more comfortable that the older, smaller version.  Bras are SUPER personal and every one of us is different, but I was hoping to share enough details and comparisons to be helpful.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seems useful. Honestly, I think this bra is worthy of shot if you liked the older version but were wishing for a bit more cup. UPDATE 6/26/2018..... I just wanted to say that I ordered the 40DD.  As I have mentioned above, I'd prefer a 38DDD or even an E... but that isn't offered in this style... so I decided to "try" the 40DD. I had feared it would be too big around, but it is actually fine... SLIGHTLY larger, but not significantly around... the "UP" part is that you get at LEAST 1/2" more in cup coverage.  To me, that's worth while.  Every woman is built uniquely and bras are SO hard, especially mail order. If you prefer a front loader... are full size..  and prefer an underwire for support, these are worth a try.  I recommend possibly trying one size around "up" if you'd like a bit more cup. I have had no issues with poor performance in wear or otherwise, however, I DO wash in a lingerie bags, on delicate and hang them to dry in my dryer "closet."  Hope this helps you in some way!

I have many pairs of skechers and because of a foot deformity I have to buy 2 different sizes - I say this because  a good fit on a pair of pull on shoes is critical. I went with my usual 8 and 9 - and they work - but I will say that the 9 seems a bit shorter at the toe than others I own.  I may have to switch up to the 9 1/2 in this style.  My feet are average in width but I feel like this shoe will work for people with a bit of a wider foot too because of the stretch top - the foot bed is not super wide - so if you have a really, really wide foot the top may work, but the bed may not be a perfect fit.  The sole is a different print than some - less spread out and deep - which is good because my favorite pair of skechers has that wide, deep sole print and it picks up all the little rocks from everywhere.  I purchased the black tops with black soles.  I personally don't like a white sole unless the tops are white or light colored.  The heel back looked a bit worrisome to me - I worried about rubbing - but it doesn't so far.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

Yes, this umbrella is a little heavier than the automatic Totes umbrella (17 ounces vs.13 ounces) but I think it will pay off in sturdiness.  The vent will be helpful in strong winds and the deeper arc will help with heavy rain. The arc on this umbrella is 46" whereas the Totes is 42". I compared the other measurements as well: this umbrella is 12 " closed and the center pole opened is 21 1/4" long whereas the Totes is 11' closed and has a 17 1/2" center pole. Both handles are about the same- 2 1/2" and 2 1/4" respectively.  The one area in which the Prostorm umbrella could be improved is the velcro strap. The Totes strap is about an inch longer and an inch wider which makes it super easy for one handed wrapping. The longer and thinner strap on the Prostorm tends to get twisted and requires a second hand to wrap it. Overall, an excellent umbrella.  ***UPDATED REVIEW***I wrote the review above before using it. IT BROKE WITHIN 5 MONTHS OF PURCHASE. THE SECOND ONE BROKE AFTER ONE USE. The auto-open button wouldn't press so the umbrella would not open. I've never had an automatic umbrella just up and stop working so I only thought I would need to review the features, not whether or not it would continue to work. Too bad because it had a lot of good features, but not the most important one- it needs to work! Guess I'll go back to TOTES.

These are the slippers of boots - totally comfortable the minute you slide your feet inside.  Sizing: I normally wear a straight-up size 6.5 in boots, really no matter the brand. I like to wear a normal-thickness pair of tight athletic socks. I almost bought a size 6 after reading some of the reviews about the boots being way too large, but I ended up going with my gut and sticking with a 6.5. I'm so glad I did - they are the perfect fit. Just enough room in the toe box with socks on. I think that if you have narrow feet, the boots might feel too loose for you around the side of the foot. I have a normal width, so they felt comfortable to me. Keep in mind that you can somewhat adjust the tightness of the boot with those side button things. They are a little difficult to work with, but it's doable. If this helps, I wear a 7 in Merrell's hiking shoes, so I somewhat sized down into my usual boot size to purchase these.  Arch support: kind of meh, but not terrible either. I'd rate it somewhere in-between. You can always swap out the insoles for your custom orthotics.  Style: intended to be somewhat comfy-slouchy, so if you're looking for a more stiffly-conforming boot, I would recommend you try a different style. These have a kind of an urban mod feel to them, which I love!

I am pleasantly surprised by the quality of this purse - especially for the price.  It comes with a tag that says it is genuine leather.. however, I am not a leather expert... it LOOKS like its leather... The bag construction is very stout.  It's thick.. stiff/sturdy - but not too heavy.  The straps are about 1" wide.  I did provide some photos so you can get a better look.  That little bump on the front is a croc head... very cute.  The flap, which has a magnetic clasp that works well and without much effort, hides a zippered closure.  Great for security and to keep things in your purse, in your purse.  The bottom is slightly rounded and even when loaded it doesn't really stand on it's own without being leaned up on something.. so that inner zipper is great.  The interior is lined with a brown satin like material - see photo.  Inside you'll find a large, wide zippered compartment and an wide slide in slot.  On the outer back of the bag there is another zippered compartment.  The straps configure to use as a backpack OR just pull up on the strap and VIOLE! it's a shoulder strap that you can actually do cross body on if you want - and I am NOT a tiny person.  The top of the strap is slightly padded - a nice thought in useful design.  There is also, as you can see in the ad photo, a sewn grab handle at the top - great for grabbing and going.  I was also pleasantly surprised at how much I could get into it.  I forgot to add a pic showing the width... it's about 3" but with a fold spread that makes it about 4 1/2".  The outer part of the bag has a new leather "sheen" to it... a little shiny... but if it is really fine leather, it will dull with use and age and take on a nice worn patina.  We'll see about that.  Honestly, if this bag had a designer sponsor, it would be a $500-$1500 bag. I have some LV - so that would be the top in number... :)  Of course it would then have their logo all over it. I should also mention it comes with a white, thin woven type dust cover bag.  I really love this bag right now. This is no junk bag. It's rich looking quality... fine, thoughtful details and price make it a trifecta of bags.  That is WIN WIN WIN. It arrived quickly (I have prime) and in excellent condition.  Having said all of that... I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seems useful.  I hope this info and the photos were helpful to you in shopping!

The peach color of this jacket is more like an electric pink or reddish pink. There is nothing peachy about it. Also the jacket is thin and made of 100% polyester. The outside feels "fleecy" but the inside has that flat polyester feel that is uncomfortable against my bare skin. The large fits snugly on me. However, it does stretch to fit even if it looks like a sausage skin on (my fault for choosing a large).  The sleeves are a good length. Oftentimes I find sleeves come above my wrists but these sleeves sit at the base of my hands as they should. The zipper easily opens and closes without the sticky feel of some zippers I have tried before. There's a "Casual Grey" logo embroidered on the front left side of the jacket. The "Casual" word is very hard to read; I only knew it said that because of the product label on Amazon.  The pockets are sewn almost completely shut and it was practically impossible to remove the stitching to open them. I had to perform "surgery" for about 15 minutes so that I could safely release the pockets. Besides this, the big negative for me is that this jacket is thin, feels cheap, and does not have that "fleecy" feel on the inside. This is the type of jacket that collects static cling in dry weather and that is not desirable in my opinion.  Had I tried this in the store, I would have left it behind on the rack.  As for care instructions, this can be machine washed on cold and hung to try.

I needed a very lightweight carryon.  I always buy the black and then I stencil with personalized print of my choice - using the oil based sharpie pens in gold and silver.  This bag spins on it's wheels easily.  I have not had the tip over issues mentioned in other reviews, but as one comment mentioned, if this happens, you just need to turn the wheels 90 degrees or so.  With any case that has spinner wheels tip over is possible if they line up just right.  The inside is surprisingly large and I find it easily holds what I need for a long weekend. It has straps inside to hold one side in place.  There are lots of  zippered compartments outside to access papers or other items you might need to... notepad, even a laptop. Just depends on how stuffed you want the bag to be. I like to keep it pretty thin in order to now have to fight it in or out of the overhead. Flying Alaska and United most of the time I have not had any issues with overhead stowing.  The handle seems sturdy. I always update with any issues that might be helpful. UPDATE:  1 31 2016.... I've added a few pics to better show the case inside and out....Also, I failed to mention that this case also offers an "extension" zip giving you about 1" or so once opened.  Remember: I personalize my black bags so I can easily spot them - when you order the bag, it will be plain black until you release your inner crafty person!  Happy travels!

Ok..I made a mistake.  This bra is NOT thinly foam lined.  It's just 2 pieces of nylon.  I wear a 38DDD.  This is a front loader - so there are not back hook adjustments.  It cuts pretty hard - so I would NOT consider this bra "all day comfort" as the tag says.  On the upside, the underwire is lifting.  But, the cup was not enough for my bust.  I went back and ordered the 40DDD to see if that helps with cup and comfort.  The do offer a 38G - but I worry about the tightness.  Listen, you do want a pretty snug fit - because the elastic will stretch.  And in this case there are no tighter hooks to go to.  But it was so uncomfortable I didn't think I could take the wait to a stretched out band.  The straps are about 3/4" wide and are adjustable. I do wish there was a thin foam lining.  Wacoal is a good brand and it appears well made.  There is a slight decor of stretch lace at the cleavage V - so it gives a bit of feminine edge.  The lace lays flat and I didn't notice it under my T shirt.  The back strap is NOT terrible wide.  So if you're afraid of "back fat" this might not be the bra for you.  It hit ok under the arm for me but I can see that some may have issues with fat there too.  All in all it's not my least favorite but I can't wear my size. U really like a front loader, but it is SUPER hard to find one built for my bust size I guess.  Even harder doing it all online.  I will check in after I get the 38G to compare the fit and update my review with that info in case if might be helpful. UPDATE... The 38G rib cage fit was a slightly more comfortable feel and the cups were deeper... but... the way the underwire is sewn it pushes down on your cleavage creating a very big cleavage bulge.. Eh.  While I really tried to make this style work, it doesn't for me.  I am returning both.  I would say that if you are DDD or larger, I would pass on this style.  Having said that.. Wacoal is a very good brand and well made.  I would try some other styles. I plan to.

LeSportsac items are beautiful.. feather weight - and yet super tough.  This backpack is no exception.  The bag is fairly small compared to other "styles" of LeSportsac.. perfect for my use.  The straps are nylon - no padded, however they are very comfortable.  The top is zippered and there is a zipped pouch on the outside.  The best part for me is that the inside lining is a lovely light beige.  This makes it so much easier to find things at the bottom.  The stitched/quilted design on the outside of the bag is beautiful. The photographs shown are accurate.  There are two handles on the top of the bag so you can easily grab the backpack like a purse if that is more convenient at the moment.  One note:  You will want to keep the top zippered closed. As the backpack doesn't really have a stable flat bottom, so when it lays down, if you have the bag open, things will fall out.  I love the convenience of backpack style bag and even though I have a substantial collection of other bags "snobby bags"  like Louie Vuitton, etc... I always come back to function. Guess that happens with age and experience! :)  LeSportsac has me hooked.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this info was useful to you!
